{"step_by_step_thinking": "The progression of bacterial infection leading to pulpal necrosis is primarily driven by the virulence of the bacteria. The documents discuss how bacteria can invade the pulp through caries or other means and cause irreversible damage. The virulence of the bacteria, which refers to their ability to cause disease and damage, is a key factor in the rapid progression of the infection. Host resistance, dentinal fluid flow, and pulp vitality are important but not the most critical factor in the rapid progression of bacterial infection leading to pulpal necrosis.", "answer_choice": "B"}
